intro: Cumulative frequency shows the running total of frequencies up to each data value. Plotting these values creates a cumulative frequency curve that lets you estimate the median, quartiles, interquartile range, and draw box plots.
explanation:
Building cumulative frequency:
Add up the frequencies as you go through the table. Each cumulative frequency value is the total number of values up to and including that group.

Plotting the curve:
Plot each cumulative frequency against the upper class boundary of each group. Connect the points with a smooth S-shaped curve. Start the curve at the lower boundary of the first class with a cumulative frequency of zero.

Reading values from the curve:
For a data set with n values:
- Median = value at n/2.
- Lower quartile (Q1) = value at n/4.
- Upper quartile (Q3) = value at 3n/4.
- Interquartile range (IQR) = Q3 - Q1.

To read a value: go across from the frequency on the y-axis to the curve, then read down to the x-axis.

Box plots:
A box plot shows the minimum, Q1, median, Q3, and maximum.
The box spans from Q1 to Q3. A vertical line inside the box marks the median. Whiskers extend from the box to the minimum and maximum values.

The IQR measures the spread of the middle 50% of data. A smaller IQR means the data is more consistent.

worked_example.question: 40 students sat a test. From the cumulative frequency curve, the values at n/4 = 10, n/2 = 20, and 3n/4 = 30 correspond to scores of 34, 48, and 57. Find the median, IQR, and describe the spread.
steps: Median = value at n/2 = 20th value = 48 marks.
Lower quartile Q1 = value at n/4 = 10th value = 34 marks.
Upper quartile Q3 = value at 3n/4 = 30th value = 57 marks.
The middle 50% of students scored within a range of 23 marks.

common_mistakes: Plotting cumulative frequencies against the midpoint of each class instead of the upper class boundary.
Reading median as n instead of n/2 — always halve the total to find which value the median is at.
Drawing straight lines between points instead of a smooth curve — a cumulative frequency graph should be a smooth S-shape.
